proof fn phi_3_execute_disable_bit63_masks_high_addr(v: usize)
    requires
        v == 0x8000_0000_0000_1000usize,
    ensures
        spec_usize2page_entry(v).perm.execute_disable == true,
        spec_usize2page_entry(v).perm.present == false,
        spec_usize2page_entry(v).addr == 0x0000_0000_0000_1000,
{
}
reason: Bit 63 is overloaded — it serves as both the execute-disable permission flag AND the sign bit for addresses, but MEM_MASK strips it from the address silently, losing potential address information

reason: Non-zero usize values (e.g. bits 3-6, 8-11 set) decode to is_empty()==true — the spec considers entries empty even when they carry non-zero hardware-reserved bits

        spec_usize2page_entry(v).perm.user == true,
        spec_usize2page_entry(v).perm.execute_disable == true,
{
}
reason: All permission bits can be simultaneously true with no constraint — the spec places no mutual exclusion between ps (hugepage) and other flags, nor validates that the combination is architecturally legal
